perturbation.original_text: Signage benefits oOne Terrace Level fascia sign. The sign is approximately 3' x 16'. SDBF to pay production costs for installation of new signage. SDBF shall install this sign on or before March 28, 2008.
perturbation.changed_text: Signage benefits oOne Terrace Level fascia sign. SDBF shall install this sign on or before March 28, 2008.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of "The sign is approximately 3' x 16'. SDBF to pay production costs for installation of new signage." introduces ambiguity regarding the size of the signage and who bears the cost of production. Without a defined size, SDBF could argue for a smaller, less prominent sign. Section 5a states that SDBF is responsible for the initial installation, however without the mention of costs the sponsorship can try to cut corners and install a smaller sign. This affects enforcement because the expected value of the signage benefit is now uncertain, creating a contradiction regarding the original intention of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SDBF shall be responsible, at its sole cost and expense, for (i) the initial installation, on or before the beginning of the Term, unless otherwise stated in the Agreement Summary, of the advertising copy in or on any signage to be furnished hereunder (the "Signage"), (ii) maintaining the fixed panel structures and (if applicable) the lighting system for the Signage and (iii) furnishing any fan giveaway items to be furnished hereunder (the "Promotional Items") (subject to any limitations with respect thereto as set forth in the Agreement Summary or agreed to by the parties) and any promotional materials prepared by SDBF with respect to the Promotional Items.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the dimensions of the sign (3' x 16') and who is responsible for production costs. The contradicted text states that SDBF is responsible for the initial installation, 'at its sole cost and expense,' of the advertising copy in or on any signage. The removal of the size and cost responsibility information in the changed text creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction because SDBF could now argue for a smaller or less costly sign than originally intended, potentially conflicting with the original agreement summary alluded to in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Hospitality benefits oSeason Tickets. SDBF shall provide Sponsor with season tickets for *** seats located in *** during the regular season of the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: Hospitality benefits oSeason Tickets. SDBF shall provide Sponsor with season tickets during the regular season of the Term.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific number and location of the seats ("*** seats located in ***"), the contract becomes ambiguous. SDBF could theoretically provide seats of significantly lower value or in undesirable locations. This contradicts the implied understanding that the season tickets would be of a certain quality, creating uncertainty in what constitutes adequate fulfillment of the 'Hospitality benefits'. This uncertainty makes Section 9 unclear, especially in the maintenance of proper decorum.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All use by Sponsor and Sponsor's invitees of any hospitality benefits granted hereunder shall be subject to the rules, regulations and policies established from time to time by SDBF and/or the Padres and may be revoked in the event of the failure of Sponsor or Sponsor's invitees to comply with such rules, regulations and policies. At the request of SDBF, Sponsor will enter into SDBF's standard-form Founders Club Agreements with respect to any Founders Club or premium seating benefits furnished hereunder. With respect to any hospitality benefits granted hereunder, Sponsor and Sponsor's invitees shall maintain proper decorum, comply with all laws, rules and regulations of all governmental authorities, not suffer or permit the continuation of any use or manner of use of the hospitality benefits in violation of any applicable Founders Club Agreements, not create any nuisance, and not take any action which either diminishes hazard insurance coverage for the Ballpark or increases the premium payable for such insurance. Sponsor and Sponsor's invitees shall be bound by and observe the terms and conditions upon which any admission tickets are issued or sold.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes specific details about the season tickets (number and location), leading to ambiguity about their value and quality. This ambiguity contradicts the contradicted section, which assumes a certain level of decorum and compliance with rules related to hospitality benefits. Without the specifics of the tickets, it's unclear what standards of decorum apply, making the enforcement of the contradicted section uncertain and potentially unenforceable. Thus, the change creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Sponsor must obtain, and continuously maintain, at its own expense, the following insurance policies: 1. Workers' Compensation in compliance with California's laws, including Employers' Liability with minimum limits of: $ *** Each Accident; $ *** Disease - Each Employee; $ *** Disease - Policy Limit. 2. An Insurance Services Office occurrence based Commercial General Liability Insurance Policy, including contractual liability and products/completed operations liability coverage with minimum limits of: $ *** Each Occurrence; $ *** General Aggregate; $ *** Products/Completed Operations Aggregate.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor must obtain, and continuously maintain, at its own expense, the following insurance policies: 1. Workers' Compensation in compliance with California's laws, including Employers' Liability. 2. An Insurance Services Office occurrence based Commercial General Liability Insurance Policy, including contractual liability and products/completed operations liability coverage.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specific insurance limits for both Workers' Compensation and Commercial General Liability introduces significant ambiguity. While the types of insurance are still required, the absence of defined limits allows Sponsor to obtain policies with minimal coverage, potentially insufficient to cover significant claims. This directly contradicts the intent of Section 8(b), which is designed to protect the Padres Parties from claims. Without specified coverage limits, it becomes difficult to enforce adequate protection, thereby introducing an internal contradiction concerning risk management.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor agrees to indemnify, protect, defend, and hold harmless SDBF, the City of San Diego, the Public Facilities Financing Authority and their respective affiliates, predecessors and successors, owners, agents, partners, officials, employees and representatives (collectively, the "Padres Parties") from and against any and all Claims (i) for libel, slander, defamation, invasion of privacy, improper trade practices, illegal competition, infringement of trademark, trade name, copyright, licenses or other proprietary rights, or unfair competition, arising from or alleged to arise from the display, publication, broadcast, telecast or distribution of any advertising copy or commercial message furnished by Sponsor Parties, or any name, brand names, trademarks, service marks, logos or other identification furnished by Sponsor Parties; (ii) arising from any promotion run in connection with this Agreement; and (iii) arising from the negligence or willful misconduct of any of Sponsor Parties. In the event that any Claim is brought against any of the Padres Parties, then, upon receipt of notification of such Claim, Sponsor will assume the defense of such Claim and, upon the request of one or more of the Padres Parties, will permit such party or parties to participate in the defense, such participation to be at such party's expense. This provision shall survive any cancellation or termination of this Agreement as to activities which occurred while this Agreement was in force.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific insurance limits for Workers' Compensation and Commercial General Liability, while the original text specifies these limits. This omission directly contradicts the intent of the contradicted text, which requires the Sponsor to indemnify, protect, defend, and hold harmless the Padres Parties from claims. The absence of specified insurance coverage limits in the changed text introduces ambiguity and makes it difficult to enforce adequate protection for the Padres Parties, creating a contradiction with the indemnification clause.
